title: Molten Crispy Gooey Russian Ackee Hash
description: A charred and gooey Russian dish featuring ackee, served in a lacto-vegetarian-friendly version.
instructions: Heat the ttukbaegi over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the mushrooms and cook until they start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the potato, tomato, paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Add the ackee and cook for 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Reduce heat to low and cook, covered, for 5-7 minutes, or until the potato is tender.
Use a spatula to create a well in the center of the ttukbaegi. Crack an egg into the well and cook until the whites are set.
Use the spatula to gently lift the edges of the hash and tilt the ttukbaegi to allow the uncooked egg to flow to the top.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the egg is cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Use the spatula to break up the hash and create a crispy, gooey crust on the bottom.
Serve the hash hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
